member.summary: The data type of the element data. See DXGI_FORMAT.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.InputElementDescription.Slot
member.summary: An integer value that identifies the input-assembler (see input slot). Valid values are between 0 and 15, defined in D3D11.h.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.InputElementDescription.AlignedByteOffset
member.summary: Optional. Offset (in bytes) from the start of the vertex. Use D3D11_APPEND_ALIGNED_ELEMENT for convenience to define the current element directly after the previous one, including any packing if necessary.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.InputElementDescription.Classification
member.summary: Identifies the input data class for a single input slot (see D3D11_INPUT_CLASSIFICATION).

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.InputElementDescription.InstanceDataStepRate
member.summary: The number of instances to draw using the same per-instance data before advancing in the buffer by one element. This value must be 0 for an element that contains per-vertex data (the slot class is set to D3D11_INPUT_PER_VERTEX_DATA).

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.MappedSubresource.DataPointer
member.summary:
Pointer to the data. When ID3D11DeviceContext::Map provides the pointer, the runtime ensures that the pointer has a specific alignment, depending on the following feature levels:
For D3D_FEATURE_LEVEL_10_0 and higher, the pointer is aligned to 16 bytes.
For lower than D3D_FEATURE_LEVEL_10_0, the pointer is aligned to 4 bytes.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RasterizerDescription.FillMode
member.summary: Determines the fill mode to use when rendering (see D3D11_FILL_MODE).

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RasterizerDescription.CullMode
member.summary: Indicates triangles facing the specified direction are not drawn (see D3D11_CULL_MODE).

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RasterizerDescription.FrontCounterClockwise
member.summary: Determines if a triangle is front- or back-facing. If this parameter is TRUE, a triangle will be considered front-facing if its vertices are counter-clockwise on the render target and considered back-facing if they are clockwise. If this parameter is FALSE, the opposite is true.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RasterizerDescription.DepthBias
member.summary: Depth value added to a given pixel. For info about depth bias, see Depth Bias.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RasterizerDescription.DepthBiasClamp
member.summary: Maximum depth bias of a pixel. For info about depth bias, see Depth Bias.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RasterizerDescription.SlopeScaledDepthBias
member.summary: Scalar on a given pixel's slope. For info about depth bias, see Depth Bias.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RasterizerDescription.DepthClipEnable
member.summary:
Enable clipping based on distance.
The hardware always performs x and y clipping of rasterized coordinates. When DepthClipEnable is set to the default–TRUE, the hardware also clips the z value (that is, the hardware performs the last step of the following algorithm).
0 < w -w <= x <= w (or arbitrarily wider range if implementation uses a guard band to reduce clipping burden) -w <= y <= w (or arbitrarily wider range if implementation uses a guard band to reduce clipping burden) 0 <= z <= w
When you set DepthClipEnable to FALSE, the hardware skips the z clipping (that is, the last step in the preceding algorithm). However, the hardware still performs the "0 < w" clipping. When z clipping is disabled, improper depth ordering at the pixel level might result. However, when z clipping is disabled, stencil shadow implementations are simplified. In other words, you can avoid complex special-case handling for geometry that goes beyond the back clipping plane.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RasterizerDescription.ScissorEnable
member.summary: Enable scissor-rectangle culling. All pixels outside an active scissor rectangle are culled.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RasterizerDescription.MultisampleEnable
member.summary: Specifies whether to use the quadrilateral or alpha line anti-aliasing algorithm on multisample antialiasing (MSAA) render targets. Set to TRUE to use the quadrilateral line anti-aliasing algorithm and to FALSE to use the alpha line anti-aliasing algorithm. For more info about this member, see Remarks.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RasterizerDescription.AntialiasedLineEnable
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable line antialiasing; only applies if doing line drawing and MultisampleEnable is FALSE. For more info about this member, see Remarks.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RasterizerDescription1.DepthClipEnable
member.summary:
Specifies whether to enable clipping based on distance.
The hardware always performs x and y clipping of rasterized coordinates. When DepthClipEnable is set to the default–TRUE, the hardware also clips the z value (that is, the hardware performs the last step of the following algorithm).
0 < w -w <= x <= w (or arbitrarily wider range if implementation uses a guard band to reduce clipping burden) -w <= y <= w (or arbitrarily wider range if implementation uses a guard band to reduce clipping burden) 0 <= z <= w
When you set DepthClipEnable to FALSE, the hardware skips the z clipping (that is, the last step in the preceding algorithm). However, the hardware still performs the "0 < w" clipping. When z clipping is disabled, improper depth ordering at the pixel level might result. However, when z clipping is disabled, stencil shadow implementations are simplified. In other words, you can avoid complex special-case handling for geometry that goes beyond the back clipping plane.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RasterizerDescription1.ScissorEnable
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable scissor-rectangle culling. All pixels outside an active scissor rectangle are culled.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RasterizerDescription1.MultisampleEnable
member.summary: Specifies whether to use the quadrilateral or alpha line anti-aliasing algorithm on multisample antialiasing (MSAA) render targets. Set to TRUE to use the quadrilateral line anti-aliasing algorithm and to FALSE to use the alpha line anti-aliasing algorithm. For more info about this member, see Remarks.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RasterizerDescription1.AntialiasedLineEnable
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable line antialiasing; only applies if doing line drawing and MultisampleEnable is FALSE. For more info about this member, see Remarks.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RasterizerDescription1.ForcedSampleCount
member.summary:
The sample count that is forced while UAV rendering or rasterizing. Valid values are 0, 1, 2, 4, 8, and optionally 16. 0 indicates that the sample count is not forced.
Note If you want to render with ForcedSampleCount set to 1 or greater, you must follow these guidelines:
Don't bind depth-stencil views.
Disable depth testing.
Ensure the shader doesn't output depth.
If you have any render-target views bound (D3D11_BIND_RENDER_TARGET) and ForcedSampleCount is greater than 1, ensure that every render target has only a single sample.
Don't operate the shader at sample frequency. Therefore, ID3D11ShaderReflection::IsSampleFrequencyShader returns FALSE. Otherwise, rendering behavior is undefined. For info about how to configure depth-stencil, see Configuring Depth-Stencil Functionality.

summary.para: Describes the blend state for a render target.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RenderTargetBlendDescription.BlendEnable
member.summary: Enable (or disable) blending.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RenderTargetBlendDescription.SourceBlend
member.summary: This blend option specifies the operation to perform on the RGB value that the pixel shader outputs. The BlendOp member defines how to combine the SrcBlend and DestBlend operations.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RenderTargetBlendDescription.DestinationBlend
member.summary: This blend option specifies the operation to perform on the current RGB value in the render target. The BlendOp member defines how to combine the SrcBlend and DestBlend operations.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RenderTargetBlendDescription.BlendOperation
member.summary: This blend operation defines how to combine the SrcBlend and DestBlend operations.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RenderTargetBlendDescription.SourceBlendAlpha
member.summary: This blend option specifies the operation to perform on the alpha value that the pixel shader outputs. Blend options that end in _COLOR are not allowed. The BlendOpAlpha member defines how to combine the SrcBlendAlpha and DestBlendAlpha operations.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RenderTargetBlendDescription.DestinationBlendAlpha
member.summary: This blend option specifies the operation to perform on the current alpha value in the render target. Blend options that end in _COLOR are not allowed. The BlendOpAlpha member defines how to combine the SrcBlendAlpha and DestBlendAlpha operations.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RenderTargetBlendDescription.BlendOperationAlpha
member.summary: This blend operation defines how to combine the SrcBlendAlpha and DestBlendAlpha operations.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RenderTargetBlendDescription.RenderTargetWriteMask
member.summary: A write mask.

summary.para: Describes the blend state for a render target.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RenderTargetBlendDescription1.BlendEnable
member.summary:
Enable (or disable) blending.
> [!NOTE] > It's not valid for *LogicOpEnable* and *BlendEnable* to both be **TRUE**.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.RenderTargetBlendDescription1.LogicOpEnable
member.summary:
Enable (or disable) a logical operation.
> [!NOTE] > If you set *LogicOpEnable* to **TRUE**, then *BlendEnable* must be **FALSE**, and the system's [**D3D11_FEATURE_DATA_D3D11_OPTIONS::OutputMergerLogicOp**](../d3d11/ns-d3d11-d3d11_feature_data_d3d11_options.md) option must be **TRUE**.

A function that compares sampled data against existing sampled data.
Lower end of the mipmap range to clamp access to, where 0 is the largest and most detailed mipmap level and any level higher than that is less detailed.

Upper end of the mipmap range to clamp access to, where 0 is the largest and most detailed mipmap level and any level higher than that is less detailed. This value must be greater than or equal to MinLOD.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SamplerDescription.Filter
member.summary: Filtering method to use when sampling a texture (see D3D11_FILTER).

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SamplerDescription.AddressU
member.summary: Method to use for resolving a u texture coordinate that is outside the 0 to 1 range (see D3D11_TEXTURE_ADDRESS_MODE).

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SamplerDescription.AddressV
member.summary: Method to use for resolving a v texture coordinate that is outside the 0 to 1 range.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SamplerDescription.AddressW
member.summary: Method to use for resolving a w texture coordinate that is outside the 0 to 1 range.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SamplerDescription.MipLODBias
member.summary: Offset from the calculated mipmap level. For example, if Direct3D calculates that a texture should be sampled at mipmap level 3 and MipLODBias is 2, then the texture will be sampled at mipmap level 5.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SamplerDescription.MaxAnisotropy
member.summary: Clamping value used if D3D11_FILTER_ANISOTROPIC or D3D11_FILTER_COMPARISON_ANISOTROPIC is specified in Filter. Valid values are between 1 and 16.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SamplerDescription.ComparisonFunc
member.summary: A function that compares sampled data against existing sampled data. The function options are listed in D3D11_COMPARISON_FUNC.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SamplerDescription.BorderColor
member.summary: Border color to use if D3D11_TEXTURE_ADDRESS_BORDER is specified for AddressU, AddressV, or AddressW. Range must be between 0.0 and 1.0 inclusive.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SamplerDescription.MinLOD
member.summary: Lower end of the mipmap range to clamp access to, where 0 is the largest and most detailed mipmap level and any level higher than that is less detailed.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SamplerDescription.MaxLOD
member.summary: Upper end of the mipmap range to clamp access to, where 0 is the largest and most detailed mipmap level and any level higher than that is less detailed. This value must be greater than or equal to MinLOD. To have no upper limit on LOD set this to a large value such as D3D11_FLOAT32_MAX.

member.summary: If the input is a texture, the D3D_RESOURCE_RETURN_TYPE-typed value that identifies the return type.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.InputBindingDescription.Dimension
member.summary: A D3D_SRV_DIMENSION-typed value that identifies the dimensions of the bound resource.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.InputBindingDescription.NumSamples
member.summary: The number of samples for a multisampled texture; when a texture isn't multisampled, the value is set to -1 (0xFFFFFFFF).

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.LibraryDescription.Creator
member.summary: The name of the originator of the library.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.LibraryDescription.Flags
member.summary: A combination of D3DCOMPILE Constants that are combined by using a bitwise OR operation. The resulting value specifies how the compiler compiles.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.LibraryDescription.FunctionCount
member.summary: The number of functions exported from the library.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ParameterDescription.Name
member.summary: The name of the function parameter.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ParameterDescription.SemanticName
member.summary: The HLSL semantic that is associated with this function parameter. This name includes the index, for example, SV_Target[n].

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ParameterDescription.Type
member.summary: A D3D_SHADER_VARIABLE_TYPE-typed value that identifies the variable type for the parameter.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ParameterDescription.Class
member.summary: A D3D_SHADER_VARIABLE_CLASS-typed value that identifies the variable class for the parameter as one of scalar, vector, matrix, object, and so on.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ParameterDescription.Rows
member.summary: The number of rows for a matrix parameter.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ParameterDescription.Columns
member.summary: The number of columns for a matrix parameter.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ParameterDescription.InterpolationMode
member.summary: A D3D_INTERPOLATION_MODE-typed value that identifies the interpolation mode for the parameter.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ParameterDescription.Flags
member.summary: A combination of D3D_PARAMETER_FLAGS-typed values that are combined by using a bitwise OR operation. The resulting value specifies semantic flags for the parameter.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ParameterDescription.FirstInRegister
member.summary: The first input register for this parameter.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ParameterDescription.FirstInComponent
member.summary: The first input register component for this parameter.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ParameterDescription.FirstOutRegister
member.summary: The first output register for this parameter.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ParameterDescription.FirstOutComponent
member.summary: The first output register component for this parameter.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ShaderParameterDescription.SemanticName
member.summary: A per-parameter string that identifies how the data will be used. For more info, see Semantics.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ShaderParameterDescription.SemanticIndex
member.summary: Semantic index that modifies the semantic. Used to differentiate different parameters that use the same semantic.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ShaderParameterDescription.Register
member.summary: The register that will contain this variable's data.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ShaderParameterDescription.SystemValueType
member.summary: A D3D_NAME-typed value that identifies a predefined string that determines the functionality of certain pipeline stages.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ShaderParameterDescription.ComponentType
member.summary: A D3D_REGISTER_COMPONENT_TYPE-typed value that identifies the per-component-data type that is stored in a register. Each register can store up to four-components of data.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ShaderParameterDescription.UsageMask
member.summary: Mask which indicates which components of a register are used.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ShaderParameterDescription.ReadWriteMask
member.summary: Mask which indicates whether a given component is never written (if the signature is an output signature) or always read (if the signature is an input signature).

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ShaderParameterDescription.Stream
member.summary: Indicates which stream the geometry shader is using for the signature parameter.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ShaderParameterDescription.MinPrecision
member.summary: A D3D_MIN_PRECISION-typed value that indicates the minimum desired interpolation precision. For more info, see Using HLSL minimum precision.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ShaderTypeDescription.Class
member.summary: A D3D_SHADER_VARIABLE_CLASS-typed value that identifies the variable class as one of scalar, vector, matrix, object, and so on.

member.summary: A D3D_SHADER_VARIABLE_TYPE-typed value that identifies the variable type.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ShaderTypeDescription.RowCount
member.summary: Number of rows in a matrix. Otherwise a numeric type returns 1, any other type returns 0.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ShaderTypeDescription.ColumnCount
member.summary: Number of columns in a matrix. Otherwise a numeric type returns 1, any other type returns 0.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ShaderTypeDescription.ElementCount
member.summary: Number of elements in an array; otherwise 0.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Shader.ShaderTypeDescription.MemberCount
member.summary: Number of members in the structure; otherwise 0.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SubresourceData.RowPitch
member.summary: The distance (in bytes) from the beginning of one line of a texture to the next line. System-memory pitch is used only for 2D and 3D texture data as it is has no meaning for the other resource types. Specify the distance from the first pixel of one 2D slice of a 3D texture to the first pixel of the next 2D slice in that texture in the SysMemSlicePitch member.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SubresourceData.SlicePitch
member.summary: The distance (in bytes) from the beginning of one depth level to the next. System-memory-slice pitch is only used for 3D texture data as it has no meaning for the other resource types.

member.name: M:Vortice.Direct3D11.Texture1DDescription.GetWidth(System.UInt32)
member.summary: Get a mip-level width.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Texture1DDescription.Width
member.summary: Texture width (in texels). The range is from 1 to D3D11_REQ_TEXTURE1D_U_DIMENSION (16384). However, the range is actually constrained by the feature level at which you create the rendering device. For more information about restrictions, see Remarks.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Texture1DDescription.MipLevels
member.summary: The maximum number of mipmap levels in the texture. See the remarks in D3D11_TEX1D_SRV. Use 1 for a multisampled texture; or 0 to generate a full set of subtextures.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Texture1DDescription.ArraySize
member.summary: Number of textures in the array. The range is from 1 to D3D11_REQ_TEXTURE1D_ARRAY_AXIS_DIMENSION (2048). However, the range is actually constrained by the feature level at which you create the rendering device. For more information about restrictions, see Remarks.

member.summary: Texture format (see DXGI_FORMAT).

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Texture1DDescription.Usage
member.summary: Value that identifies how the texture is to be read from and written to. The most common value is D3D11_USAGE_DEFAULT; see D3D11_USAGE for all possible values.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Texture1DDescription.BindFlags
member.summary: Flags (see D3D11_BIND_FLAG) for binding to pipeline stages. The flags can be combined by a logical OR. For a 1D texture, the allowable values are: D3D11_BIND_SHADER_RESOURCE, D3D11_BIND_RENDER_TARGET and D3D11_BIND_DEPTH_STENCIL.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Texture1DDescription.CPUAccessFlags
member.summary: Flags (see D3D11_CPU_ACCESS_FLAG) to specify the types of CPU access allowed. Use 0 if CPU access is not required. These flags can be combined with a logical OR.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Texture1DDescription.MiscFlags
member.summary: Flags (see D3D11_RESOURCE_MISC_FLAG) that identify other, less common resource options. Use 0 if none of these flags apply. These flags can be combined with a logical OR.

For more information about restrictions, see Remarks.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Texture2DDescription.MipLevels
member.summary: The maximum number of mipmap levels in the texture. See the remarks in D3D11_TEX1D_SRV. Use 1 for a multisampled texture; or 0 to generate a full set of subtextures.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Texture2DDescription.ArraySize
member.summary: Number of textures in the texture array. The range is from 1 to D3D11_REQ_TEXTURE2D_ARRAY_AXIS_DIMENSION (2048). For a texture cube-map, this value is a multiple of 6 (that is, 6 times the value in the NumCubes member of D3D11_TEXCUBE_ARRAY_SRV), and the range is from 6 to 2046. The range is actually constrained by the feature level at which you create the rendering device. For more information about restrictions, see Remarks.

member.summary: Value that identifies how the texture is to be read from and written to. The most common value is D3D11_USAGE_DEFAULT; see D3D11_USAGE for all possible values.

member.summary: Flags (see D3D11_BIND_FLAG) for binding to pipeline stages. The flags can be combined by a logical OR.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Texture2DDescription.CPUAccessFlags
member.summary: Flags (see D3D11_CPU_ACCESS_FLAG) to specify the types of CPU access allowed. Use 0 if CPU access is not required. These flags can be combined with a logical OR.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Texture2DDescription.MiscFlags
member.summary: Flags (see D3D11_RESOURCE_MISC_FLAG) that identify other, less common resource options. Use 0 if none of these flags apply. These flags can be combined by using a logical OR. For a texture cube-map, set the D3D11_RESOURCE_MISC_TEXTURECUBE flag. Cube-map arrays (that is, ArraySize > 6) require feature level D3D_FEATURE_LEVEL_10_1 or higher.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Texture2DDescription1.MiscFlags
member.summary: Flags (see D3D11_RESOURCE_MISC_FLAG) that identify other, less common resource options. Use 0 if none of these flags apply. These flags can be combined by using a logical OR. member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Texture2DDescription1.TextureLayout
member.summary:
A D3D11_TEXTURE_LAYOUT-typed value that identifies the layout of the texture.
The TextureLayout parameter selects both the actual layout of the texture in memory and the layout visible to the application while the texture is mapped. These flags may not be requested without CPU access also requested.
It is illegal to set CPU access flags on default textures without also setting TextureLayout to a value other than D3D11_TEXTURE_LAYOUT_UNDEFINED.
D3D11_TEXTURE_LAYOUT_ROW_MAJOR may only be used to create non-multisampled, textures with a single subresource (Planar YUV textures are supported). These textures may only be used as a source and destination of copy operations, and BindFlags must be zero.
D3D11_TEXTURE_LAYOUT_64K_STANDARD_SWIZZLE may only be used to create non-multisampled, non-depth-stencil textures.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.Texture3DDescription1.TextureLayout
member.summary:
A D3D11_TEXTURE_LAYOUT-typed value that identifies the layout of the texture.
The TextureLayout parameter selects both the actual layout of the texture in memory and the layout visible to the application while the texture is mapped. These flags may not be requested without CPU access also requested.
It is illegal to set CPU access flags on default textures without also setting Layout to a value other than D3D11_TEXTURE_LAYOUT_UNDEFINED.
D3D11_TEXTURE_LAYOUT_ROW_MAJOR may not be used with 3D textures. D3D11_TEXTURE_LAYOUT_64K_STANDARD_SWIZZLE may not be used with 3D textures that have mipmaps.

member.name: T:Vortice.Direct3D11.TiledResourceCoordinate
member.summary: Describes the coordinates of a tiled resource.

summary.para: Describes the coordinates of a tiled resource.

member.name: M:Vortice.Direct3D11.TiledResourceCoordinate.#ctor(System.UInt32,System.UInt32,System.UInt32,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct.
member.param: The x position of a tiled resource. Used for buffer and 1D, 2D, and 3D textures.
The y position of a tiled resource. Used for 2D and 3D textures.
The z position of a tiled resource. Used for 3D textures.

A subresource index value into mipmaps and arrays. Used for 1D, 2D, and 3D textures. For mipmaps that use nonstandard tiling, or are packed, or both use nonstandard tiling and are packed, any subresource value that indicates any of the packed mipmaps all refer to the same tile.

member.summary: The x position of a tiled resource. Used for buffer and 1D, 2D, and 3D textures.

member.summary: The y position of a tiled resource. Used for 2D and 3D textures.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.TiledResourceCoordinate.Z
member.summary: The z position of a tiled resource. Used for 3D textures.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.TiledResourceCoordinate.Subresource
member.summary:
A subresource index value into mipmaps and arrays. Used for 1D, 2D, and 3D textures.
For mipmaps that use nonstandard tiling, or are packed, or both use nonstandard tiling and are packed, any subresource value that indicates any of the packed mipmaps all refer to the same tile.

member.summary: Describes the size of a tiled region.

summary.para: Describes the size of a tiled region.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct.
member.param: The number of tiles in the tiled region.

member.name: M:Vortice.Direct3D11.TileRegionSize.#ctor(System.UInt32,System.UInt16,System.UInt16)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.TileRegionSize.NumTiles
member.summary: The number of tiles in the tiled region.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.TileRegionSize.UseBox
member.summary:
Specifies whether the runtime uses the Width, Height, and Depth members to define the region.
If TRUE, the runtime uses the Width, Height, and Depth members to define the region.
If FALSE, the runtime ignores the Width, Height, and Depth members and uses the NumTiles member to traverse tiles in the resource linearly across x, then y, then z (as applicable) and then spills over mipmaps/arrays in subresource order. For example, use this technique to map an entire resource at once.
Regardless of whether you specify TRUE or FALSE for bUseBox, you use a D3D11_TILED_RESOURCE_COORDINATE structure to specify the starting location for the region within the resource as a separate parameter outside of this structure by using x, y, and z coordinates.
When the region includes mipmaps that are packed with nonstandard tiling, bUseBox must be FALSE because tile dimensions are not standard and the app only knows a count of how many tiles are consumed by the packed area, which is per array slice. The corresponding (separate) starting location parameter uses x to offset into the flat range of tiles in this case, and y and z coordinates must each be 0.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.TileRegionSize.Width
member.summary: The width of the tiled region, in tiles. Used for buffer and 1D, 2D, and 3D textures.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.TileRegionSize.Height
member.summary: The height of the tiled region, in tiles. Used for 2D and 3D textures.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.TileRegionSize.Depth
member.summary: The depth of the tiled region, in tiles. Used for 3D textures or arrays. For arrays, used for advancing in depth jumps to next slice of same mipmap size, which isn't contiguous in the subresource counting space if there are multiple mipmaps.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.AuthenticatedConfigureInput.Omac
member.summary: A D3D11_OMAC structure that contains a Message Authentication Code (MAC) of the data. The driver uses AES-based one-key CBC MAC (OMAC) to calculate this value for the block of data that appears after this structure member.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.AuthenticatedConfigureInput.ConfigureType
member.summary:
A GUID that specifies the command. The following GUIDs are defined.
Value Meaning
D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_CONFIGURE_CRYPTO_SESSION
Associates a cryptographic session with a decoder device and a Direct3D device.
Input data: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_CONFIGURE_CRYPTO_SESSION_INPUT
D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_CONFIGURE_ENCRYPTION_WHEN_ACCESSIBLE
Sets the level of encryption that is performed before protected content becomes accessible to the CPU or bus.
Input data: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_CONFIGURE_ACCESSIBLE_ENCRYPTION_INPUT
D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_CONFIGURE_INITIALIZE
Initializes the authenticated channel.
Input data: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_CONFIGURE_INITIALIZE_INPUT
D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_CONFIGURE_PROTECTION
Enables or disables protection for the device.
Input data: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_CONFIGURE_PROTECTION_INPUT
D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_CONFIGURE_SHARED_RESOURCE
Enables a process to open a shared resource, or disables a process from opening shared resources.
Input data: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_CONFIGURE_SHARED_RESOURCE_INPUT

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.AuthenticatedConfigureInput.Channel
member.summary: A handle to the authenticated channel. To get the handle, call the ID3D11AuthenticatedChannel::GetChannelHandle method.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.AuthenticatedConfigureInput.SequenceNumber
member.summary: The query sequence number. At the start of the session, generate a cryptographically secure 32-bit random number to use as the starting sequence number. For each query, increment the sequence number by 1.

member.summary:
A D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_PROCESS_IDENTIFIER_TYPE value that specifies the type of process.
To specify the Desktop Window Manager (DWM) process, set this member to D3D11_PROCESSIDTYPE_DWM. Otherwise, set this member to D3D11_PROCESSIDTYPE_HANDLE and set the ProcessHandle member to a valid handle.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.AuthenticatedConfigureSharedResourceInput.ProcessHandle
member.summary: A process handle. If the ProcessType member equals D3D11_PROCESSIDTYPE_HANDLE, the ProcessHandle member specifies a handle to a process. Otherwise, the value is ignored.

summary.para: Contains input data for the ID3D11VideoContext::QueryAuthenticatedChannel method.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.AuthenticatedQueryInput.QueryType
member.summary:
A GUID that specifies the query. The following GUIDs are defined.
Value Meaning
D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_ACCESSIBILITY_ATTRIBUTES
Returns the type of I/O bus that is used to send data to the GPU.
Output data structure: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_ACCESSIBILITY_OUTPUT
D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_CHANNEL_TYPE
Returns the type of authenticated channel.
Output data structure: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_CHANNEL_TYPE_OUTPUT
D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_CRYPTO_SESSION
Returns handles to the cryptographic session and Direct3D device that are associated with a specified decoder device.
Input data structure: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_CRYPTO_SESSION_INPUT
Output data structure: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_CRYPTO_SESSION_OUTPUT
D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_CURRENT_ENCRYPTION_WHEN_ACCESSIBLE
Returns the encryption type that is applied before content becomes accessible to the CPU or bus.
Output data structure: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_ACCESSIBILITY_ENCRYPTION_GUID_COUNT_OUTPUT
D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_DEVICE_HANDLE
Returns a handle to the device that is associated with this authenticated channel.
Output data structure: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_DEVICE_HANDLE_OUTPUT
D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_ENCRYPTION_WHEN_ACCESSIBLE_GUID
Returns one of the encryption types that can be used to encrypt content before it becomes accessible to the CPU or bus.
Input data structure: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_ACCESSIBILITY_ENCRYPTION_GUID_INPUT
Output data structure: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_ACCESSIBILITY_ENCRYPTION_GUID_OUTPUT
D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_ENCRYPTION_WHEN_ACCESSIBLE_GUID_COUNT
Returns the number of encryption types that can be used to encrypt content before it becomes accessible to the CPU or bus.
Output data structure: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_ACCESSIBILITY_ENCRYPTION_GUID_COUNT_OUTPUT
D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_OUTPUT_ID
Returns one of the output identifiers that is associated with a specified cryptographic session and Direct3D device.
Input data structure: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_OUTPUT_ID_INPUT
Output data structure: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_OUTPUT_ID_OUTPUT
D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_OUTPUT_ID_COUNT
Returns the number of output identifiers that are associated with a specified cryptographic session and Direct3D device.
Input data structure: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_OUTPUT_ID_COUNT_INPUT
Output data structure: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_OUTPUT_ID_COUNT_OUTPUT
D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_PROTECTION
Returns the current protection level for the device.
Output data structure: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_PROTECTION_OUTPUT
D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_RESTRICTED_SHARED_RESOURCE_PROCESS
Returns information about a process that is allowed to open shared resources with restricted access.
Input data structure: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_RESTRICTED_SHARED_RESOURCE_PROCESS_INPUT
Output data structure: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_RESTRICTED_SHARED_RESOURCE_PROCESS_OUTPUT
D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_RESTRICTED_SHARED_RESOURCE_PROCESS_COUNT
Returns the number of processes that are allowed to open shared resources with restricted access.
Output data structure: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_RESTRICTED_SHARED_RESOURCE_PROCESS_COUNT_OUTPUT
D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_UNRESTRICTED_PROTECTED_SHARED_RESOURCE_COUNT
Returns the number of protected shared resources that can be opened by any process with no restrictions.
Output data structure: D3D11_AUTHENTICATED_QUERY_UNRESTRICTED_PROTECTED_SHARED_RESOURCE_COUNT_OUTPUT

summary.para: Describes information about Direct3D 11.1 adapter architecture.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataArchitectureInfo.TileBasedDeferredRenderer
member.summary: Specifies whether a rendering device batches rendering commands and performs multipass rendering into tiles or bins over a render area. Certain API usage patterns that are fine for TileBasedDefferredRenderers (TBDRs) can perform worse on non-TBDRs and vice versa. Applications that are careful about rendering can be friendly to both TBDR and non-TBDR architectures. TRUE if the rendering device batches rendering commands and FALSE otherwise.

summary.para: Describes compute shader and raw and structured buffer support in the current graphics driver.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D10XHardwareOptions.ComputeShadersPlusRawAndStructuredBuffersViaShader4X
member.summary: TRUE if compute shaders and raw and structured buffers are supported; otherwise FALSE.

summary.para: Describes Direct3D 11.1 feature options in the current graphics driver.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options.OutputMergerLogicOp
member.summary: Specifies whether logic operations are available in blend state. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if logic operations are available in blend state and FALSE otherwise. This member is FALSE for feature level 9.1, 9.2, and 9.3. This member is optional for feature level 10, 10.1, and 11. This member is TRUE for feature level 11.1.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options.UAVOnlyRenderingForcedSampleCount
member.summary: Specifies whether the driver can render with no render target views (RTVs) or depth stencil views (DSVs), and only unordered access views (UAVs) bound. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the driver can render with no RTVs or DSVs and only UAVs bound and FALSE otherwise. If TRUE, you can set the ForcedSampleCount member of D3D11_RASTERIZER_DESC1 to 1, 4, or 8 when you render with no RTVs or DSV and only UAVs bound. For feature level 11.1, this member is always TRUE and you can also set ForcedSampleCount to 16 in addition to 1, 4, or 8. The default value of ForcedSampleCount is 0, which means the same as if the value is set to 1. You can always set ForcedSampleCount to 0 or 1 for UAV-only rendering independently of how this member is set.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options.DiscardAPIsSeenByDriver
member.summary: Specifies whether the driver supports the ID3D11DeviceContext1::DiscardView and ID3D11DeviceContext1::DiscardResource methods. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the driver supports these methods and FALSE otherwise. How this member is set does not indicate whether the driver actually uses these methods; that is, the driver might ignore these methods if they are not useful to the hardware. If FALSE, the runtime does not expose these methods to the driver because the driver does not support them. You can monitor this member during development to rule out legacy drivers on hardware where these methods might have otherwise been beneficial. You are not required to write separate code paths based on whether this member is TRUE or FALSE; you can call these methods whenever applicable.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options.FlagsForUpdateAndCopySeenByDriver
member.summary: Specifies whether the driver supports new semantics for copy and update that are exposed by the ID3D11DeviceContext1::CopySubresourceRegion1 and ID3D11DeviceContext1::UpdateSubresource1 methods. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the driver supports new semantics for copy and update. The runtime sets this member to FALSE only for legacy drivers. The runtime handles this member similarly to the DiscardAPIsSeenByDriver member.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options.ClearView
member.summary:
Specifies whether the driver supports the ID3D11DeviceContext1::ClearView method. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the driver supports this method and FALSE otherwise. If FALSE, the runtime does not expose this method to the driver because the driver does not support it.
Note For feature level 9.1, 9.2, and 9.3, this member is always TRUE because the option is emulated by the runtime.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options.CopyWithOverlap
member.summary:
Specifies whether you can call ID3D11DeviceContext1::CopySubresourceRegion1 with overlapping source and destination rectangles. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if you can call CopySubresourceRegion1 with overlapping source and destination rectangles and FALSE otherwise. If FALSE, the runtime does not expose this method to the driver because the driver does not support it.
Note For feature level 9.1, 9.2, and 9.3, this member is always TRUE because drivers already support the option for these feature levels.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options.ConstantBufferPartialUpdate
member.summary:
Specifies whether the driver supports partial updates of constant buffers. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the driver supports partial updates of constant buffers and FALSE otherwise. If FALSE, the runtime does not expose this operation to the driver because the driver does not support it.
Note For feature level 9.1, 9.2, and 9.3, this member is always TRUE because the option is emulated by the runtime.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options.ConstantBufferOffsetting
member.summary:
Specifies whether the driver supports new semantics for setting offsets in constant buffers for a shader. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the driver supports allowing you to specify offsets when you call new methods like the ID3D11DeviceContext1::VSSetConstantBuffers1 method and FALSE otherwise. If FALSE, the runtime does not expose this operation to the driver because the driver does not support it.
Note For feature level 9.1, 9.2, and 9.3, this member is always TRUE because the option is emulated by the runtime.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options.MapNoOverwriteOnDynamicConstantBuffer
member.summary:
Specifies whether you can call ID3D11DeviceContext::Map with D3D11_MAP_WRITE_NO_OVERWRITE on a dynamic constant buffer (that is, whether the driver supports this operation). The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the driver supports this operation and FALSE otherwise. If FALSE, the runtime fails this method because the driver does not support the operation.
Note For feature level 9.1, 9.2, and 9.3, this member is always TRUE because the option is emulated by the runtime.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options.MapNoOverwriteOnDynamicBufferSRV
member.summary: Specifies whether you can call ID3D11DeviceContext::Map with D3D11_MAP_WRITE_NO_OVERWRITE on a dynamic buffer SRV (that is, whether the driver supports this operation). The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the driver supports this operation and FALSE otherwise. If FALSE, the runtime fails this method because the driver does not support the operation.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options.MultisampleRTVWithForcedSampleCountOne
member.summary: Specifies whether the driver supports multisample rendering when you render with RTVs bound. If TRUE, you can set the ForcedSampleCount member of D3D11_RASTERIZER_DESC1 to 1 with a multisample RTV bound. The driver can support this option on feature level 10 and higher. If FALSE, the rasterizer-state creation will fail because the driver is legacy or the feature level is too low.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options.SAD4ShaderInstructions
member.summary: Specifies whether the hardware and driver support the msad4 intrinsic function in shaders. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the hardware and driver support calls to msad4 intrinsic functions in shaders. If FALSE, the driver is legacy or the hardware does not support the option; the runtime will fail shader creation for shaders that use msad4.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options.ExtendedDoublesShaderInstructions
member.summary: Specifies whether the hardware and driver support the fma intrinsic function and other extended doubles instructions (DDIV and DRCP) in shaders. The fma intrinsic function emits an extended doubles DFMA instruction. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the hardware and driver support extended doubles instructions in shaders (shader model 5 and higher). Support of this option implies support of basic double-precision shader instructions as well. You can use the D3D11_FEATURE_DOUBLES value to query for support of double-precision shaders. If FALSE, the hardware and driver do not support the option; the runtime will fail shader creation for shaders that use extended doubles instructions.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options.ExtendedResourceSharing
member.summary: Specifies whether the hardware and driver have [extended support for shared Texture2D resource types and formats](/windows/win32/direct3d11/direct3d-11-1-features#extended-support-for-shared-texture2d-resources). The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the hardware and driver support extended Texture2D resource sharing.

summary.para: Describes Direct3D 11.2 feature options in the current graphics driver.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options1.TiledResourcesTier
member.summary: Specifies whether the hardware and driver support tiled resources. The runtime sets this member to a D3D11_TILED_RESOURCES_TIER-typed value that indicates if the hardware and driver support tiled resources and at what tier level.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options1.MinMaxFiltering
member.summary: Specifies whether the hardware and driver support the filtering options (D3D11_FILTER) of comparing the result to the minimum or maximum value during texture sampling. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the hardware and driver support these filtering options.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options1.ClearViewAlsoSupportsDepthOnlyFormats
member.summary: Specifies whether the hardware and driver also support the ID3D11DeviceContext1::ClearView method on depth formats. For info about valid depth formats, see D3D11_DEPTH_STENCIL_VIEW_DESC.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options1.MapOnDefaultBuffers
member.summary: Specifies support for creating ID3D11Buffer resources that can be passed to the ID3D11DeviceContext::Map and ID3D11DeviceContext::Unmap methods. This means that the CPUAccessFlags member of the D3D11_BUFFER_DESC structure may be set with the desired D3D11_CPU_ACCESS_FLAG elements when the Usage member of D3D11_BUFFER_DESC is set to D3D11_USAGE_DEFAULT. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the hardware is capable of at least D3D_FEATURE_LEVEL_11_0 and the graphics device driver supports mappable default buffers.

summary.para: Describes Direct3D 11.3 feature options in the current graphics driver.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options2.PSSpecifiedStencilRefSupported
member.summary: Specifies whether the hardware and driver support PSSpecifiedStencilRef. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the hardware and driver support this option.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options2.TypedUAVLoadAdditionalFormats
member.summary: Specifies whether the hardware and driver support TypedUAVLoadAdditionalFormats. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the hardware and driver support this option.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options2.ROVsSupported
member.summary: Specifies whether the hardware and driver support ROVs. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the hardware and driver support this option.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options2.ConservativeRasterizationTier
member.summary: Specifies whether the hardware and driver support conservative rasterization. The runtime sets this member to a D3D11_CONSERVATIVE_RASTERIZATION_TIER-typed value that indicates if the hardware and driver support conservative rasterization and at what tier level.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options2.TiledResourcesTier
member.summary: Specifies whether the hardware and driver support tiled resources. The runtime sets this member to a D3D11_TILED_RESOURCES_TIER-typed value that indicates if the hardware and driver support tiled resources and at what tier level.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options2.MapOnDefaultTextures
member.summary: Specifies whether the hardware and driver support mapping on default textures. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the hardware and driver support this option.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options2.StandardSwizzle
member.summary: Specifies whether the hardware and driver support standard swizzle. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the hardware and driver support this option.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options2.UnifiedMemoryArchitecture
member.summary: Specifies whether the hardware and driver support Unified Memory Architecture. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the hardware and driver support this option.

summary.para: Describes Direct3D 11.3 feature options in the current graphics driver.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options3.VPAndRTArrayIndexFromAnyShaderFeedingRasterizer
member.summary: Whether to use the VP and RT array index from any shader feeding the rasterizer.

summary.para: Describes Direct3D 11.4 feature options in the current graphics driver.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options4.ExtendedNV12SharedTextureSupported
member.summary: Specifies a BOOL that determines if NV12 textures can be shared across processes and D3D devices.

summary.para: Describes the level of support for shared resources in the current graphics driver.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D11Options5.SharedResourceTier
member.summary: A shared resource support tier.

summary.para: Describes Direct3D 9 feature options in the current graphics driver.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D9Options.FullNonPow2TextureSupport
member.summary: Specifies whether the driver supports the nonpowers-of-2-unconditionally feature. For more information about this feature, see feature level. The runtime sets this member to TRUE for hardware at Direct3D 10 and higher feature levels. For hardware at Direct3D 9.3 and lower feature levels, the runtime sets this member to FALSE if the hardware and driver support the powers-of-2 (2D textures must have widths and heights specified as powers of two) feature or the nonpowers-of-2-conditionally feature. For more information about this feature, see feature level.

summary.para: Describes Direct3D 9 feature options in the current graphics driver.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D9Options1.FullNonPow2TextureSupported
member.summary: Specifies whether the driver supports the nonpowers-of-2-unconditionally feature. For more info about this feature, see feature level. The runtime sets this member to TRUE for hardware at Direct3D 10 and higher feature levels. For hardware at Direct3D 9.3 and lower feature levels, the runtime sets this member to FALSE if the hardware and driver support the powers-of-2 (2D textures must have widths and heights specified as powers of two) feature or the nonpowers-of-2-conditionally feature.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D9Options1.DepthAsTextureWithLessEqualComparisonFilterSupported
member.summary: Specifies whether the driver supports the shadowing feature with the comparison-filtering mode set to less than or equal to. The runtime sets this member to TRUE for hardware at Direct3D 10 and higher feature levels. For hardware at Direct3D 9.3 and lower feature levels, the runtime sets this member to TRUE only if the hardware and driver support the shadowing feature; otherwise FALSE.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D9Options1.SimpleInstancingSupported
member.summary: Specifies whether the hardware and driver support simple instancing. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the hardware and driver support simple instancing.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D9Options1.TextureCubeFaceRenderTargetWithNonCubeDepthStencilSupported
member.summary:
Specifies whether the hardware and driver support setting a single face of a TextureCube as a render target while the depth stencil surface that is bound alongside can be a Texture2D (as opposed to TextureCube). The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the hardware and driver support this feature; otherwise FALSE.
If the hardware and driver don't support this feature, the app must match the render target surface type with the depth stencil surface type. Because hardware at Direct3D 9.3 and lower feature levels doesn't allow TextureCube depth surfaces, the only way to render a scene into a TextureCube while having depth buffering enabled is to render each TextureCube face separately to a Texture2D render target first (because that can be matched with a Texture2D depth), and then copy the results into the TextureCube. If the hardware and driver support this feature, the app can just render to the TextureCube faces directly while getting depth buffering out of a Texture2D depth buffer.
You only need to query this feature from hardware at Direct3D 9.3 and lower feature levels because hardware at Direct3D 10.0 and higher feature levels allow TextureCube depth surfaces.

summary.para: Describes Direct3D 9 shadow support in the current graphics driver.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D9ShadowSupport.SupportsDepthAsTextureWithLessEqualComparisonFilter
member.summary: Specifies whether the driver supports the shadowing feature with the comparison-filtering mode set to less than or equal to. The runtime sets this member to TRUE for hardware at Direct3D 10 and higher feature levels. For hardware at Direct3D 9.3 and lower feature levels, the runtime sets this member to TRUE only if the hardware and driver support the shadowing feature; otherwise FALSE.

summary.para: Describes whether simple instancing is supported.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataD3D9SimpleInstancingSupport.SimpleInstancingSupported
member.summary: Specifies whether the hardware and driver support simple instancing. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the hardware and driver support simple instancing.

summary.para: Describes double data type support in the current graphics driver.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataDoubles.DoublePrecisionFloatShaderOps
member.summary: Specifies whether double types are allowed. If TRUE, double types are allowed; otherwise FALSE. The runtime must set DoublePrecisionFloatShaderOps to TRUE in order for you to use any HLSL shader that is compiled with a double type.

summary.para: Describes which resources are supported by the current graphics driver for a given format.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataFormatSupport.InFormat
member.summary: DXGI_FORMAT to return information on.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataFormatSupport.OutFormatSupport
member.summary: Combination of D3D11_FORMAT_SUPPORT flags indicating which resources are supported.

summary.para: Describes which unordered resource options are supported by the current graphics driver for a given format.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataFormatSupport2.InFormat
member.summary: DXGI_FORMAT to return information on.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataFormatSupport2.OutFormatSupport2
member.summary: Combination of D3D11_FORMAT_SUPPORT2 flags indicating which unordered resource options are supported.

summary.para: Describes feature data GPU virtual address support, including maximum address bits per resource and per process.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataGpuVirtualAddressSupport.MaxGPUVirtualAddressBitsPerResource
member.summary: The maximum GPU virtual address bits per resource.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataGpuVirtualAddressSupport.MaxGPUVirtualAddressBitsPerProcess
member.summary: The maximum GPU virtual address bits per process.

summary.para: Describes whether a GPU profiling technique is supported.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataMarkerSupport.Profile
member.summary: Specifies whether the hardware and driver support a GPU profiling technique that can be used with development tools. The runtime sets this member to TRUE if the hardware and driver support data marking.

summary.para: Describes the level of shader caching supported in the current graphics driver.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataShaderCache.SupportFlags
member.summary: Indicates the level of caching supported.

summary.para: Describes precision support options for shaders in the current graphics driver.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataShaderMinPrecisionSupport.PixelShaderMinPrecision
member.summary: A combination of D3D11_SHADER_MIN_PRECISION_SUPPORT-typed values that are combined by using a bitwise OR operation. The resulting value specifies minimum precision levels that the driver supports for the pixel shader. A value of zero indicates that the driver supports only full 32-bit precision for the pixel shader.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataShaderMinPrecisionSupport.AllOtherShaderStagesMinPrecision
member.summary: A combination of D3D11_SHADER_MIN_PRECISION_SUPPORT-typed values that are combined by using a bitwise OR operation. The resulting value specifies minimum precision levels that the driver supports for all other shader stages. A value of zero indicates that the driver supports only full 32-bit precision for all other shader stages.

summary.para: Describes the multi-threading features that are supported by the current graphics driver.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataThreading.DriverConcurrentCreates
member.summary: TRUE means resources can be created concurrently on multiple threads while drawing; FALSE means that the presence of coarse synchronization will prevent concurrency.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataThreading.DriverCommandLists
member.summary: TRUE means command lists are supported by the current driver; FALSE means that the API will emulate deferred contexts and command lists with software.

summary.para: Provides data for calls to ID3D11VideoDevice2::CheckFeatureSupport when the feature specified is D3D11_FEATURE_VIDEO_DECODER_HISTOGRAM.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataVideoDecoderHistogram.DecoderDescription
member.summary: A [D3D11_VIDEO_DECODER_DESC](../d3d11/ns-d3d11-d3d11_video_decoder_desc.md) structure containing the decoder description for the decoder to be used with decode histogram.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataVideoDecoderHistogram.Components
member.summary: A bitwise OR combination of values from the [D3D11_VIDEO_DECODER_HISTOGRAM_COMPONENT_FLAGS](ne-d3d11_4-d3d11_video_decoder_histogram_component_flags.md) enumeration specifying the components of a DXGI_FORMAT for which histogram support will be queried.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataVideoDecoderHistogram.BinCount
member.summary: The number of per component bins supported. This value must be greater than or equal to 64 and must be a power of 2 (e.g. 64, 128, 256, 512...).

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.FeatureDataVideoDecoderHistogram.CounterBitDepth
member.summary: The bit depth of the bin counter. The counter is always stored in a 32-bit value and therefore this value must specify 32 bits or less. The counter is stored in the lower bits of the 32-bit storage. The upper bits are set to zero. If the bin count exceeds this bit depth, the value is set to the maximum counter value. Valid values for *CounterBitDepth* are 16, 24, and 32.

summary.para: Represents key exchange data for hardware content protection.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.KeyExchangeHwProtectionData.HWProtectionFunctionID
member.summary: The function ID of the DRM command. The values and meanings of the function ID are defined by the DRM specification.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.KeyExchangeHwProtectionData.InputData
member.summary: Pointer to a buffer containing a D3D11_KEY_EXCHANGE_HW_PROTECTION_INPUT_DATA structure that specifies memory reserved for IHV use and the input data for the DRM command.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.KeyExchangeHwProtectionData.OutputData
member.summary: Pointer to a buffer containing a D3D11_KEY_EXCHANGE_HW_PROTECTION_OUTPUT_DATA structure that specifies memory reserved for IHV use and the input data for the DRM command.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.KeyExchangeHwProtectionData.Status
member.summary: The result of the hardware DRM command.

summary.para: Represents key exchange input data for hardware content protection.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.KeyExchangeHwProtectionInputData.PrivateDataSize
member.summary: The size of the private data reserved for IHV usage. This size is determined from the pPrivateInputSize parameter returned by the ID3D11VideoDevice1::GetCryptoSessionPrivateDataSize function.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.KeyExchangeHwProtectionInputData.HWProtectionDataSize
member.summary: The size of the DRM command data.

member.name: P:Vortice.Direct3D11.KeyExchangeHwProtectionInputData.PbInput
member.summary:
If PrivateDataSize is greater than 0, pbInput[0] – pbInput[PrivateDataSize - 1] is reserved for IHV use.
pbInput[PrivateDataSize] – pbInput[HWProtectionDataSize + PrivateDataSize - 1] contains the input data for the DRM command. The format and size of the DRM command is defined by the DRM specification.

summary.para: Represents key exchange output data for hardware content protection.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.KeyExchangeHwProtectionOutputData.PrivateDataSize
member.summary: The size of the private data reserved for IHV usage. This size is determined from the pPrivateOutputSize parameter returned by the ID3D11VideoDevice1::GetCryptoSessionPrivateDataSize function.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.KeyExchangeHwProtectionOutputData.MaxHWProtectionDataSize
member.summary: The maximum size of data that the driver can return in the output buffer. The last byte that it can write to is pbOuput[PrivateDataSize + MaxHWProtectionDataSize – 1].

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.KeyExchangeHwProtectionOutputData.HWProtectionDataSize
member.summary: The size of the output data written by the driver.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.KeyExchangeHwProtectionOutputData.TransportTime
member.summary: The number of 100 nanosecond units spent transporting the data.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.KeyExchangeHwProtectionOutputData.ExecutionTime
member.summary: The number of 100 nanosecond units spent executing the content protection command.

member.name: P:Vortice.Direct3D11.KeyExchangeHwProtectionOutputData.PbOutput
member.summary:
If PrivateDataSize is greater than 0, pbInput[0] – pbOutput[PrivateDataSize - 1] is reserved for IHV use.
pbOutput[PrivateDataSize] – pbOutput[HWProtectionDataSize + PrivateDataSize - 1] contains the input data for the DRM command. The format and size of the DRM command is defined by the DRM specification.

member.name: P:Vortice.Direct3D11.Omac.Buffer
member.summary: A byte array that contains the cryptographic MAC value of the message.

summary.para: Describes the tile structure of a tiled resource with mipmaps.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.PackedMipDescription.NumStandardMips
member.summary: Number of standard mipmaps in the tiled resource.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.PackedMipDescription.NumPackedMips
member.summary:
Number of packed mipmaps in the tiled resource.
This number starts from the least detailed mipmap (either sharing tiles or using non standard tile layout). This number is 0 if no such packing is in the resource. For array surfaces, this value is the number of mipmaps that are packed for a given array slice where each array slice repeats the same packing.
On Tier_2 tiled resources hardware, mipmaps that fill at least one standard shaped tile in all dimensions are not allowed to be included in the set of packed mipmaps. On Tier_1 hardware, mipmaps that are an integer multiple of one standard shaped tile in all dimensions are not allowed to be included in the set of packed mipmaps. Mipmaps with at least one dimension less than the standard tile shape may or may not be packed. When a given mipmap needs to be packed, all coarser mipmaps for a given array slice are considered packed as well.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.PackedMipDescription.NumTilesForPackedMips
member.summary:
Number of tiles for the packed mipmaps in the tiled resource.
If there is no packing, this value is meaningless and is set to 0. Otherwise, it is set to the number of tiles that are needed to represent the set of packed mipmaps. The pixel layout within the packed mipmaps is hardware specific. If apps define only partial mappings for the set of tiles in packed mipmaps, read and write behavior is vendor specific and undefined. For arrays, this value is only the count of packed mipmaps within the subresources for each array slice.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.PackedMipDescription.StartTileIndexInOverallResource
member.summary:
Offset of the first packed tile for the resource in the overall range of tiles. If NumPackedMips is 0, this value is meaningless and is 0. Otherwise, it is the offset of the first packed tile for the resource in the overall range of tiles for the resource. A value of 0 for StartTileIndexInOverallResource means the entire resource is packed. For array surfaces, this is the offset for the tiles that contain the packed mipmaps for the first array slice. Packed mipmaps for each array slice in arrayed surfaces are at this offset past the beginning of the tiles for each array slice.
Note The number of overall tiles, packed or not, for a given array slice is simply the total number of tiles for the resource divided by the resource's array size, so it is easy to locate the range of tiles for any given array slice, out of which StartTileIndexInOverallResource identifies which of those are packed.

summary.para: Query information about graphics-pipeline activity in between calls to ID3D11DeviceContext::Begin and ID3D11DeviceContext::End.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.QueryDataPipelineStatistics.IAVertices
member.summary: Number of vertices read by input assembler.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.QueryDataPipelineStatistics.IAPrimitives
member.summary: Number of primitives read by the input assembler. This number can be different depending on the primitive topology used. For example, a triangle strip with 6 vertices will produce 4 triangles, however a triangle list with 6 vertices will produce 2 triangles.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.QueryDataPipelineStatistics.VSInvocations
member.summary: Number of times a vertex shader was invoked. Direct3D invokes the vertex shader once per vertex.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.QueryDataPipelineStatistics.GSInvocations
member.summary: Number of times a geometry shader was invoked. When the geometry shader is set to NULL, this statistic may or may not increment depending on the hardware manufacturer.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.QueryDataPipelineStatistics.GSPrimitives
member.summary: Number of primitives output by a geometry shader.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.QueryDataPipelineStatistics.CInvocations
member.summary: Number of primitives that were sent to the rasterizer. When the rasterizer is disabled, this will not increment.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.QueryDataPipelineStatistics.CPrimitives
member.summary: Number of primitives that were rendered. This may be larger or smaller than CInvocations because after a primitive is clipped sometimes it is either broken up into more than one primitive or completely culled.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.QueryDataPipelineStatistics.PSInvocations
member.summary: Number of times a pixel shader was invoked.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.QueryDataPipelineStatistics.HSInvocations
member.summary: Number of times a hull shader was invoked.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.QueryDataPipelineStatistics.DSInvocations
member.summary: Number of times a domain shader was invoked.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.QueryDataPipelineStatistics.CSInvocations
member.summary: Number of times a compute shader was invoked.

summary.para: Query information about the amount of data streamed out to the stream-output buffers in between ID3D11DeviceContext::Begin and ID3D11DeviceContext::End.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.QueryDataSOStatistics.NumPrimitivesWritten
member.summary: Number of primitives (that is, points, lines, and triangles) written to the stream-output buffers.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.QueryDataSOStatistics.PrimitivesStorageNeeded
member.summary: Number of primitives that would have been written to the stream-output buffers if there had been enough space for them all.

summary.para: Query information about the reliability of a timestamp query.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.QueryDataTimestampDisjoint.Frequency
member.summary: How frequently the GPU counter increments in Hz.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.QueryDataTimestampDisjoint.Disjoint
member.summary: If this is TRUE, something occurred in between the query's ID3D11DeviceContext::Begin and ID3D11DeviceContext::End calls that caused the timestamp counter to become discontinuous or disjoint, such as unplugging the AC cord on a laptop, overheating, or throttling up/down due to laptop savings events. The timestamp returned by ID3D11DeviceContext::GetData for a timestamp query is only reliable if Disjoint is FALSE.

summary.para: Description of a vertex element in a vertex buffer in an output slot.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SODeclarationEntry.Stream
member.summary: Zero-based, stream number.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SODeclarationEntry.SemanticName
member.summary: Type of output element; possible values include: "POSITION", "NORMAL", or "TEXCOORD0". Note that if SemanticName is NULL then ComponentCount can be greater than 4 and the described entry will be a gap in the stream out where no data will be written.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SODeclarationEntry.SemanticIndex
member.summary: Output element's zero-based index. Should be used if, for example, you have more than one texture coordinate stored in each vertex.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SODeclarationEntry.StartComponent
member.summary: Which component of the entry to begin writing out to. Valid values are 0 to 3. For example, if you only wish to output to the y and z components of a position, then StartComponent should be 1 and ComponentCount should be 2.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SODeclarationEntry.ComponentCount
member.summary: The number of components of the entry to write out to. Valid values are 1 to 4. For example, if you only wish to output to the y and z components of a position, then StartComponent should be 1 and ComponentCount should be 2. Note that if SemanticName is NULL then ComponentCount can be greater than 4 and the described entry will be a gap in the stream out where no data will be written.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SODeclarationEntry.OutputSlot
member.summary: The associated stream output buffer that is bound to the pipeline (see ID3D11DeviceContext::SOSetTargets). The valid range for OutputSlot is 0 to 3.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SubresourceTiling.WidthInTiles
member.summary: The width in tiles of the subresource.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SubresourceTiling.HeightInTiles
member.summary: The height in tiles of the subresource.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SubresourceTiling.DepthInTiles
member.summary: The depth in tiles of the subresource.

member.name: F:Vortice.Direct3D11.SubresourceTiling.StartTileIndexInOverallResource
member.summary:
The index of the tile in the overall tiled subresource to start with.
GetResourceTiling sets StartTileIndexInOverallResource to D3D11_PACKED_TILE (0xffffffff) to indicate that the whole D3D11_SUBRESOURCE_TILING structure is meaningless, and the info to which the pPackedMipDesc parameter of GetResourceTiling points applies. For packed tiles, the description of the packed mipmaps comes from a D3D11_PACKED_MIP_DESC structure instead.
